_description: Actual match outcomes and per-question RBP results from the JTC SportsPredict platform
actual_score: Argentina 3-2 Cape Verde (after extra time -- regulation ended level)

question_results.text: Will Lionel Messi (Argentina) have 3 or more shots on target?
Will a goal be scored before the first hydration break?
Will Lautaro Martínez (Argentina) score a goal?
Will Argentina keep a clean sheet?
question_results.note: Argentina conceded 2 -- part of the Cape Verde near-miracle context (regulation ended level, forcing ET).
Both Elo (536pt gap) and market (85.5%) agreed and were both wrong about the regulation-time result specifically -- see JULY3_POSTMORTEM_DEEP_DIVE.md section 6.

question_results.text: Will Cape Verde have 2 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: RULE15 blowout-suppression shaded this down from market's 0.51; Cape Verde was NOT in blowout-victim mode, they were playing the game of their tournament. Their Uruguay-game (competitive) precedent should have been weighted over their Spain-game (blowout-victim) precedent.

question_results.text: Will Argentina score 3 or more goals?
Will Argentina score in both halves?
Will Argentina have 8 or more shots on target?
Will Julián Álvarez (Argentina) score or assist?
question_results.note: WORST RESULT OF MATCH. Illiquid market (offer 65%) trusted too much despite his 0G/0A group-stage record and bench role in 2 of 3 games; crowd's 39% was much closer. See JULY3_POSTMORTEM_DEEP_DIVE.md section 5.

question_results.text: Will there be 3 or more total cards shown?
Will Argentina score the first goal?
Will Argentina have 8 or more corner kicks?
Will Argentina be ahead at halftime?
Will there be 22 or more total shots?

key_lessons: The whole match's shape is explained by one fact: regulation ended level (Cape Verde forced ET). Q4/Q5/Q6 losses all trace back to this single tail event materializing despite two independent, cross-validated pre-match signals (Elo + market) both being confident in Argentina.
Biggest loss: Julián Álvarez score-or-assist (-29.25) -- illiquid-market overtrust on a personally out-of-form favorite-side player. Directly informed the PERSONAL_ZERO_OVERRIDES_TEAM_CONTEXT rule.

Biggest win: Argentina 8+ corners (+23.36) -- correctly priced below crowd despite Argentina being a heavy favorite; shows the favorite-SOT-underpricing pattern does not automatically extend to corners.
